sensitivity: 85.6% (95% CI: 82.6 - 88.7%) for murmur classification; 96.2% (95% CI: 94.9 - 97.4%) for S1 detection; 92.3% (95% CI: 90.3 - 94.3%) for S2 detection
intended_use: The Eko Murmur Analysis Software is intended to provide decision support to clinicians in their evaluation of patients' heart sounds. The software analyzes heart sounds and phonocardiograms (and ECG s
intended_use_full: The Eko Murmur Analysis Software is intended to provide decision support to clinicians in their evaluation of patients' heart sounds. The software analyzes heart sounds and phonocardiograms (and ECG signals, when available). The software will automatically detect murmurs that may be present, and the murmur timing and character, including S1, S2, innocent heart murmurs, structural heart murmurs, and the absence of a heart murmur.
ground_truth: Multiple cardiologist annotations paired with gold standard echocardiogram for murmur classification; cardiologist annotations for S1/S2 timing (299 recordings); cardiologists were blinded to demographic data and echocardiogram findings
other_metrics: Signal quality classification (good vs poor); murmur timing classification (systolic vs diastolic)
